lines.text: The Hospital, Phoebe is arriving with Ross, Joey, and Rachel in tow.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (to the nurse) Hi.

lines.speaker: Nurse
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi, yeah, hi! I'm umm, Phoebe Buffay, and I have babies coming out of me.

lines.speaker: Nurse
lines.text: Okay. Have you started having contractions?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Not yet. Umm, I heard they really hurt, do they hurt?

lines.speaker: Nurse
lines.text: Well...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It's all right.

lines.speaker: Nurse
lines.text: Now, which of you is the father? (Points to Joey and Ross)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh no, none of them are the father. The father is my brother.

lines.speaker: Nurse
lines.text: (not sure what to do with that) Okay...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I am so gonna miss watching you freak people out like that!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, uh Pheebs, quick. Look! This (His video camera) is for the babies to look at someday, so is-is there anything you want to say? Y'know before it all starts?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, okay, umm, hi kids! Um, it's me, Aunt Phoebe. I can't wait to see you. Please don't hurt me!
Monica and Chandler come running in.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, what took you guys so long? Your cab left when ours did!
There's a pause as they figure out what to say.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, we-we had to go back because I forget my jacket.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's right.
Both Rachel and Ross stare at her for a moment.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You-you're not wearing a jacket.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh man! I did it again!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (standing up) Okay, so umm, somebody has to call Frank and Alice. (As she is talking Joey is sticking the camera under her skirt.) And then my mom wants to know-(notices Joey)-Joey, what are you doing?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I gotta get the before shot!
She shakes her head no.
Opening Credits
The delivery room, Rachel and Ross are entering.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi, Pheebs? Okay, so just spoke to the nurse and the reason that your doctor is late is because uh, she's not coming.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Apparently she fell in the shower and hit her head.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God, she's so stupid!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, Pheebs-Pheebs, it's gonna be okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That's easy for you to say, I don't see three kids coming out your vagina!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Honey, listen, y'know what? The nurse said the doctor is wonderful.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, he's head of the department.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right-Ooh! Oh dead God, save me!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm having my first contraction!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh no.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh, it's not bad.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh! (In an announcer type voice) And so the miracle of life begins, and aaiiyyyeeee! (He grabs his side and doubles over in pain.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey! You okay?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ooh, something hurts!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh, it's sympathy pains. Ohh, that's so sweet!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Are they? I didn't know I cared that much.
The doctor, Dr. Harad, enters.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Dr. Harad
lines.text: Hi! Phoebe, I'm Dr. Harad, I'm going to be delivering your babies. I want you to know, you're gonna be in good hands. I've been doing this for a long time. I'll be back in a minute to do your internal, in the meantime, just relax because everything here looks great. And also, I love Fonzie. (Exits)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Did he just say, he loves Fonzie?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's what it sounded like.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right...

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: (entering) Hey!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: (To Phoebe) Hey! Am I late? Am I late? Nobody came out yet, right?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No-no-no! We haven't started yet. Where's Alice?

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Uh, Delaware. She's on her way though, so until she gets here, I'm gonna be your coach. But don't worry, she told me all about the la-Mazada stuff.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, that's when if you get the babies out by the end of the month, they give you 2% financing.

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Yeah.
The waiting room, Monica and Joey are sitting there.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (entering) Monica? You gonna be very proud of me. I just got us dates with two unbelievably cute nurses.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh my!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: They're male nurses.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Not in my head.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Anyway, they want to take us out Saturday night! What do you say?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Umm. (Looks at Chandler who is using the phone.) Umm. Umm. I don't think so.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What? What are you talking about?! You-you're the one who's been telling me to get over Ross and move on. I'm moving on, and you're moving on with me. Come on, give me one good reason why you don't wanna go.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Umm, why don't you give me something that would be a good reason and-and then I'll tell you if it's true.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Harder than it sounds. Isn't it?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, you're coming with me, and I also told them that if we're still here when they get off that we'll go down to the cafeteria and have some Jell-O with them.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (in a sexy voice) Yep! There's always room for Jell-O...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, how do you make that dirty?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, it's easy. Yeah, I-I can do it with anything. Watch uh, (snaps his fingers and in a sexy voice) Grandma's chicken salad...
The delivery room, Chandler, Frank, and Ross are with Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering) Ross! Get a shot of this. (He's carrying an issue of the USA Today and hands Ross the camera.) Hey babies! These are the headlines on the day you were born! Okay, now girl baby turn away and boy babies... (Throws the paper away to reveal a copy of Playpen, which is the TV version of Playboy Magazine.) Check it out, huh?! This is what naked women looked like the month you were born. All right, now let's dive right into the good stuff. (Joey opens the magazine and Ross sticks the camera in it.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, okay, I'm having another one! This one doesn't hurt either-Ooh, yes it does! Ow! Ow! Ow! Ow! Ooh! (Checks under the blanket.) Oh, I was kinda hoping that was it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, where are Monica and Rachel anyway?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, a couple of nurses asked them out. Maybe they're with them.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Really? Male nurses?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, I was bummed too.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So they're going on dates? When?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I think Saturday-(groans in pain again).

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: (To Phoebe) What's with him?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Umm, sympathy pains. I thought it was really sweet at first, but now I think he's just trying to steal my thunder.

lines.speaker: Dr. Harad
lines.text: (entering) Okay!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Dr. Harad
lines.text: Let's see what we got here. Ohh, y'know, Fonzie dated triplets.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: This-this Fonzie person you keep referring too, is that uh, is that another doctor?

lines.speaker: Dr. Harad
lines.text: Oh no-no-no. Fonzie is the nickname of Arthur Fonzerelli. The Fonz.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right.
Dr. Harad exits.

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: It's not that weird, is it?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's very weird! I don't want some guy down there telling me, I'm y'know, dilatedamundo!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: To be fair, he doesn't seem to be impersonating Fonzie...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (interrupting) What are you doing?!! Why are you defending him?! Just get me another doctor! One who is not crazy and who is not Fonzie!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Again, it's not that he...
Phoebe fakes pain to get Ross looking for another doctor.
The waiting room, Chandler is looking for Monica.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (spotting her) Oh-hey-hey-hey! There you are!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Umm, listen there's something I think you should know.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, is this about you-you dating the nurse? Yeah, Joey already told me, and I am so-so fine. I mean, you and I we're just, y'know, we're nothing, we're goofin' around.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Umm, actually I was about to tell you that I was, I was going to get out of it, but hey, if we're just goofing around then uh, maybe I will go out with him.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Fine! Maybe I will too!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering from the elevator caring gifts for the kids) Hey, you guys! Look what I found in the giiiiiiift shop. (He doubles over in pain in front an old man in a wheel chair.) Get up! Get up! Get up! (The old man waves him away.)
The delivery room, Ross has returned with another doctor. This one, is well, younger.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, Phoebe, this Dr. Oberman. He has no strong feelings about Fonzie or any of the Happy Days gang.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi! And you're going into what grade?

lines.speaker: Dr. Oberman
lines.text: Umm, I'm actually a first year resident, but I get that a lot, you see, I-I graduated early...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (interrupting) Uh-huh, me too. Ross, maybe I should've specified that I'd be needing a grown up doctor.

lines.speaker: Dr. Oberman
lines.text: Oh no, I'm fully qualified to...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Shh! Doogie, shh! Doesn't anybody understand that I'm gonna be having babies soon? Huh? Go! Go little boy, go!
He runs out and Frank watches him go.

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Oh cool! You made him cry!
Another hospital room, Joey has now been admitted and his doctor is about to break the bad news to him, Monica, and Chandler.

lines.speaker: Joey's Doctor
lines.text: Mr. Tribbiani, I'm afraid you've got kidney stones.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Umm, well, what else could it be?

lines.speaker: Joey's Doctor
lines.text: It's kidney stones.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Or?

lines.speaker: Joey's Doctor
lines.text: Kidney stones!
The delivery room, Dr. Harad is back and checking on Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Dr. Harad
lines.text: All right, you're getting there. Oh, and y'know, these babies are very, very lucky.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: They are. Why?

lines.speaker: Dr. Harad
lines.text: They have the honor of being born on The Fonz's half-birthday.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Happy birthday!

lines.speaker: Dr. Harad
lines.text: Just-just to clarify, I'm not Fonzie. (Phoebe nods in agreement as he leaves.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Honey, y'know I just gotta tell you, I think this is such a terrific thing you're having these babies for Frank and Alice.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know, it is.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Can I tell you a little secret?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I want to keep one. (Giggles in excitement.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh, I'm gonna be on the news!
Commercial Break
The delivery room, continued from earlier.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, Phoebe, honey, you gotta be kidding. I mean, you know you cannot keep one of these babies!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why not?! Maybe I can, you don't know!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes! Yes! Yes, I do! I do know! Frank and Alice are gonna want to keep all of their children!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Maybe not! Y'know? Seriously, three babies are a handful maybe they're y'know, looking for a chance to unload one of them. Listen, I-I hate to miss an opportunity just because I didn't ask! Y'know?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Phoebe, no! This is, this is insane.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, just ask him!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Me?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I can't ask him! Do you have any idea how inappropriate that would be?! All I'm saying is just talk to Frank. Okay? Just, y'know, feel him out!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No! Forget it! I am not gonna ask Frank to give you one of his kids!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You're right.
There's an awkward silence then suddenly Phoebe gets an idea.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Tell him it's for you.
Joey's room, his doctor, Chandler, and Monica are there.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Feeling a little better sweetie?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, maybe a little. I wish you hadn't seen me throw up.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Me too.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering) Hey! I just heard. What's up?

lines.speaker: Joey's Doctor
lines.text: Kidney stones! Now, ordinarily Mr. Tribbiani, we try to break up the stones up with shock waves, but they're to close to the bladder now. Which means we can either wait for you to pass them or else go up the urethra...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (interrupting) Whoa-whoa! No-no-no-no-no, nothing is going up! Okay? Up, up is not an option-what's a urethra? (Monica whispers what it is in his ear.) Are you crazy?!
The delivery room, Rachel and Frank are there.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (To Rachel) So did you ask him?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, I haven't had a chance to be alone with him yet.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I'm kinda on a clock here.

lines.speaker: Dr. Harad
lines.text: (laughing) Oh Fonzie.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Y'know who I always liked? Mork.
Dr. Harad drops what he's doing and stares at her.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Undo it. Undo it. Undo it.

lines.speaker: Dr. Harad
lines.text: Fonzie met Mork. Mork froze Fonzie.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, but umm... Yes, but, Fonzie was already cool, so he wasn't hurt, right?

lines.speaker: Dr. Harad
lines.text: Yeah, that's right.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (entering with Chandler) Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (To Phoebe) How are you doing?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, doctor says any minute now.

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Hey, y'know, Alice is gonna be here so soon, you couldn't just like do me a favor and like, like hold them in?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Sorry Frank, I'm kinda in the middle of the last favor you asked me to do.
A male nurse enters.

lines.speaker: Male Nurse
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Male Nurse
lines.text: Rachel.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Monica, this is Dan (points to him), one of the guys that we're gonna be going out with on Saturday. (Mouths "He's yours." to her.) Uh Dan, Monica.

lines.speaker: Dan
lines.text: Nice to meet you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hello Dan! I'm really looking forward to Saturday night! Really, really!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So Dan, nurse not a doctor huh? Kinda girlie isn't it?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chandler!

lines.speaker: Dan
lines.text: Nah that's okay. I'm just doing this to put myself through medical school.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Dan
lines.text: And it didn't feel so girlie during the Gulf War.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Sure. (Pause) And listen, thanks for doing that for us, by the way. (Retreats in defeat.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So, why wait 'til Saturday, are you free tomorrow?

lines.speaker: Dan
lines.text: Sure! I'll get somebody to cover my shift.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, great!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to another female nurse) Hey, how 'bout it? You, me, Saturday night?

lines.speaker: Delivery Room Nurse
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right. Very good.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oo, this is a big one. Eww! Arghhhh!!
[Cut to Joey's room, who's going throw his own contractions. Plus, he has Ross in a headlock.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ohh, get these things out of me!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Breathe! Breathe! Breathe throw the pain.
Joey starts breathing hard

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I want the drugs Ross, I want the drugs! (He starts rocking back and forth, taking Ross with him.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I do too! I do too!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Argh!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Argh!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Argh!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Argh!
The waiting room, Frank is on the phone as Rachel approaches.

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Yeah, I love you. Okay, bye! (To Rachel) Hi!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: That was Alice's mom, she said she left five hours ago. She should be here by now!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, honey, don't worry. She's gonna make it on time.

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah. So Frank, three babies. Whew, that just seems like a lot, huh?

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: (laughs) Not to me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, fair enough.
The delivery room, later on, Rachel, Chandler, Monica, and Frank are there as Dr. Harad is checking out Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Dr. Harad
lines.text: Okay, you're at ten centimeters. Time to start having some babies. All right, I want only the father in here please.
They all kiss her and wish her luck.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Bye Dan!

lines.speaker: Dan
lines.text: Uh, bye Monica.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Bye, momi-moo.
Everyone except Frank leaves.

lines.speaker: Dr. Harad
lines.text: All right, I need a clamp, sterile towel, and channel 31.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What is that?
Dan turns on the TV and the Happy Days theme song comes on.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Dr. Harad
lines.text: Oh, no-no-no, it's a good one! Fonzie plays the bongos. All right, are you ready? It's time to start pushing.
[Cut to Joey's room, his doctor and Ross are there.]

lines.speaker: Joey's Doctor
lines.text: Are you ready? It's time to try peeing. (Joey makes a face like he is trying to pee.) Wait-wait-wait-wait-wait! It's almost time to try peeing. (Points at the bottle Joey is to pee into.)
[Cut to the delivery room.]

lines.speaker: Dr. Harad
lines.text: Okay, now push! That's it push! Just concentrate on pushing! Yeah, here we go!

lines.speaker: Dan
lines.text: I see the head.

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Yes, it has a head!

lines.speaker: Dr. Harad
lines.text: All right. Keep pushing! Come on!

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: I can't believe there's somebody coming out of you right now. There's somebody coming out of you! Is it? Is it? It's my son.

lines.speaker: Dr. Harad
lines.text: All right. Here's your first baby.
[Cut to the waiting room, a triumphant Frank rushes in.]

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: YESSSSS!!!!! We got a baby boy!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes!

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Frank Jr. Jr.!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, how does he look? How does he look?

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: So gross! (He runs back to the delivery room.)
[Cut to the delivery room, Phoebe is about to give birth to the middle kid.]

lines.speaker: Dr. Harad
lines.text: Okay. You ready to push again?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I already had a baby. Leave me alone.

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Okay, okay, come on, you can do it. You can do it!
Phoebe screams in pain, and Frank screams with her.
[Cut to the waiting room, a twice triumphant Frank returns.]

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Yeah!! Little Leslie is here! We got another one! Oh my God, I can't believe I have two-two children. How scary is that? (He returns to the delivery room.)
[Cut to the delivery room, Phoebe is at it for the last time.]

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Come on little Chandler, it's time to be born. Come on little Chandler! Come on!

lines.speaker: Dr. Harad
lines.text: All right, he's coming. He's coming!
They both stare at the newborn.

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Hey, where's his thing?
[Cut to the waiting room, a thrice triumphant Frank returns again.]

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Chandler's a girl!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh God, kindergarten flashback.

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: They musta read the sonogram wrong. 'Cause they, 'cause they thought it was a boy, but Chandler's a girl! Chandler's a girl!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, keep saying it!

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: (running in from the elevator) Am I too late?!

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: No-no ah, everything's okay. Everybody's healthy there's 30 fingers and 30 toes.

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: We have our babies?

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: (Starting to cry) Oh, we have our babies.
They hug. And quickly that hug turns into a heated make out session, right there on the waiting room couch. Chandler, Rachel, and Monica quickly make their exits.
Joey's room, he is recovering from his birth.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh my God.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You did it, man.

lines.speaker: Joey's Doctor
lines.text: Would you like to see them? (He hands Joey a little jar.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: They're so small! (Both he and Ross look at them with satisfied looks on their faces.)
A hallway, Monica and Dan are talking.

lines.speaker: Dan
lines.text: So, I'll call you tomorrow.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Great!
Dan leaves as Chandler enters.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So, are you really gonna go out with that nurse man?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well uh, you and I are just goofing around, I thought, why not goof around with him.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know, I don't know if you've ever looked up the term goofing around in the dictionary... Well, I have, and the technical definition is, two friends who care a lot about each other and have amazing sex and just wanna spend more time together. But if you have this new fangled dictionary that gets you made at me, then we have to, y'know, get you my original dictionary. I am so bad at this.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I think you're better than you think you are.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Really? Okay, so...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (interrupting) Know when to stop.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know, I sensed that I should stop. So we're okay?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah. All right, I'm gonna go tell Dan that it's not gonna happen. (They kiss and as she starts to leave, Chandler starts to dance. Without turning around.) Don't do the dance.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Right!
The delivery room, everyone except Rachel is there. Phoebe, Monica, and Ross are holding the babies.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I think you're my favorite.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Which one do you have?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don't care.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (entering) Hi. (To Phoebe) Hey, hi! So uh, Frank and Alice wanted me to tell you that they're still outside making phone calls.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: But umm, I mean, did you talk to them about, y'know...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, umm, no honey.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh. It was a long shot. Hey, you guys can I just like have a second alone with the babies.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Yeah, sure yeah. Yeah.
They hand her the babies and leave them alone.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So, here you are. It seems like yesterday I was talking to you in that little petri dish. Everyone said labor was the hardest thing I'd ever have to do, but they were wrong this is. Oh, I had the most fun with you guys! I wish I could take you home and see you everyday. Okay, I'll settle for being your favorite Aunt. I know Alice's sister has a pool, but you lived in me. Okay, so we're cool. Yeah, we're gonna be great. Little high fives! (Imitates the high fives.) Ahh! Ahh! Ahh! (One of the babies begins to cry.) Well, if you're gonna cry. (She starts crying.)
Ending Credits
The delivery room, everyone is hanging out with Phoebe. Frank, Alice, and the kids aren't there.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe, we are so proud of you! You're amazing!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So does it really hurt as bad as they say?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah. You won't be able to take it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So uh, now that little Chandler turned out to be a girl, what are they gonna name her?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: They're gonna call her Chandler.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's kind of a masculine name, don't you think?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Works on you.
End
